medications.name: Levothyroxine
medications.frequency: Once daily — fasting, 30 mins before breakfast
medications.caution: Do NOT take with iron supplements or calcium — take 4 hours apart

medications.name: Ferrous Sulphate
medications.caution: Take 4 hours AFTER Levothyroxine. Causes dark stools — normal.

medications.name: Labetalol
medications.indication: Hypertension — alpha and beta blocker, safe for women
medications.caution: Do not stop abruptly. Monitor for bradycardia.

visits.chiefComplaint: Fatigue, cold intolerance, weight gain, heavy periods
visits.clinicalNote: Patient presents with classical hypothyroid symptoms — fatigue, cold intolerance, weight gain of 4kg over 3 months, constipation. TSH 8.2 — significantly elevated. Initiating Levothyroxine 50mcg. Haemoglobin critically low at 8.4 — iron deficiency anaemia confirmed with ferritin 6. Starting Ferrous Sulphate 200mg twice daily. IMPORTANT: Counselled patient to take iron and thyroid medications 4 hours apart — absorption conflict. No known drug allergies. BP borderline 135/85 — monitoring.
visits.plan: Recheck TSH in 3 months. Haemoglobin in 3 months. BP monitoring at each visit.

visits.chiefComplaint: BP elevated on two consecutive readings
visits.clinicalNote: BP 142/90 at this visit — second elevated reading. Meets criteria for gestational hypertension. Starting Labetalol 100mg twice daily — safe choice for women. TSH 6.1 improving on Levothyroxine — dose increased to 75mcg. Haemoglobin 9.8 — improving. Patient reports she has been taking iron and thyroid tablet together in the morning — re-counselled on timing. Drug interaction: Levothyroxine + Ferrous Sulphate if taken together reduces absorption by up to 50%.
visits.plan: Levothyroxine dose increased to 75mcg. BP review in 4 weeks. Continue iron therapy.

visits.clinicalNote: Excellent treatment response. TSH 3.8 — now within normal range. Haemoglobin 11.2 — near normal, significant recovery. BP responding to Labetalol — 130/82. Patient reports improved energy, reduced fatigue. Ferritin 28 — iron stores recovering. Cross-referring to Cardiology for BP optimisation and long-term cardiovascular monitoring.
visits.plan: Continue all medications. Cardiology cross-referral. Recheck TSH + Hb in 3 months.

visits.chiefComplaint: Cardiology cross-referral from Dr. Sharmila — BP management
visits.clinicalNote: Received cross-referral from Dr. Sharmila Ganesan. BP now 132/84 — reasonable control on Labetalol. TSH slight rise to 4.8 — may need Levothyroxine dose review, referred back to OB/GYN. Haemoglobin 11.6 — slight dip from 12.1. Drug timing conflict noted again in review: patient admitted to sometimes taking iron and thyroid tablet together — counselled again on strict 4-hour separation. No chest symptoms. ECG normal. Continue current regimen with closer monitoring.
visits.plan: Continue Labetalol. OB/GYN review for Levothyroxine dose. Monitor Hb. Repeat ECG in 6 months.

clinicalFlags.flag: TSH rising — possible dose review needed
clinicalFlags.evidence: TSH rose from 2.1 (Dec 2025) to 4.8 (Feb 2026) after normalising
clinicalFlags.recommendation: Consider Levothyroxine dose adjustment — for physician review

clinicalFlags.evidence: Patient repeatedly noted to take both tablets together — documented 3x
clinicalFlags.recommendation: Strict 4-hour separation counselling. Absorption up to 50% reduced if taken together — for physician review
Monitor. Continue iron supplementation — for physician review

overdueTests.reason: Haemoglobin monitoring — every 3 months on iron therapy
